channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CFL Player Dies in Fatal Crash Involving SUV Driver Going the Wrong Way
item.description: A fatal crash involving a Saskatchewan Roughriders player has raised concerns about road safety and substance use. RCMP confirmed that the driver of a silver SUV was traveling the wrong way on a divid...

item.title: Ariel Winter Returns as Voice of Sofia in New Disney Series
item.description: Ariel Winter, the voice behind Disney's 'Sofia the First,' is back for a new spin-off series titled 'Sofia the First: Royal Magic.' The show, premiering on Disney Jr. and Disney+, follows Sofia as she...

item.title: Carnapper Injured in Accident Involving Stolen License Plate
item.description: A carnapper, identified as Christopher Sitson of Barangay San Isidro, Antipolo, Rizal, was involved in a traffic incident on July 21, 2026. According to ABS-CBN News, the individual sustained injuries...

item.title: NAFDAC takes action against Onifam Laboratories for regulatory breaches
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) has officially blacklisted Onifam Laboratories due to severe regulatory violations. The agency accused the company of misusing...

item.title: Andy Burnham becomes UK's seventh prime minister in ten years following Labour leadership change
item.description: The article reports on Andy Burnham's ascension as the UK's seventh prime minister within a decade, following Keir Starmer's loss of confidence in his Labour Party leadership. Burnham, former mayor of...

item.title: Government Maintains No Plans to Expand Ethanol Blending Beyond 20%
item.description: The Indian government has clarified that no decision has been taken to increase ethanol blending in petrol beyond the 20% threshold. Minister of State for Petroleum and Natural Gas Suresh Gopi stated ...

item.title: DOJ and DHS Deny FBI Will Cease Investigating ICE-Related Shootings
item.description: The article reports allegations that the FBI has informed its managers it will no longer investigate shootings involving Department of Homeland Security agents, including ICE. This purported policy sh...

item.title: FIA Launches Cryptocurrency Unit to Combat Financial Crimes
item.description: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) has established a cryptocurrency investigation unit within its National Command and Control Centre (NC3) to address money laundering and terrorism financing thro...

item.title: Army Veteran Accused of Incendiary Attack on NYC Federal Building with Anti-ICE Materials
item.description: An Army veteran, Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was arrested after allegedly setting fire to the Jacob K. Javits Federal Office Building in Lower Manhattan with gasoline and a firework canister. Authorities rep...

item.title: Iran and Pakistan Focus on Peace and Security in Bilateral Talks
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan's new ambassador Imran Ahmed Siddiqui held discussions in Tehran, emphasizing peace and security as central to bilateral relations. The meeting occ...

item.title: Pakistani Politician Accuses Army of Concealing Kargil War Casualties
item.description: Maulana Fazal-ur-Rehman, president of the Jamiat Ulema-e-Islam, accused the Pakistan Army of concealing casualties during the 1999 Kargil War. He claimed that Islamabad did not publicly release the li...

item.title: Family finds closure as Chinnadu's killer is convicted in 'car noise' murder case
item.description: Kubendran Sholan Chinnadu, a 29-year-old electrician from Northdale, was tragically shot and killed in Pietermaritzburg in July 2020 following a dispute over 'car noise'. The incident occurred when Ch...

item.title: Philippines and US Discuss Regional Cooperation Ahead of ASEAN Meetings
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. received United States Ambassador to India Sergio Gor and US Ambassador to the Philippines Lee Lipton at Malacañang Palace ahead of ASEAN-related engagements in Manila. ...

item.title: Five family members, including a Utah fire captain, killed in flash flooding near Bicknell
item.description: Five family members, including Provo fire captain Spencer Long and his wife Katrina, along with their three sons Reid, Thayne, and Gage, tragically died in a flash flood near Bicknell, Utah. The incid...

item.title: Trump's Qatari jet security concerns emerge after conflicting statements and legal actions
item.description: Jen Psaki, a journalist for MS NOW, highlighted President Donald Trump's inconsistent statements regarding the security of his Qatari-gifted Air Force One. Initially, Trump denied any safety concerns ...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates Amid Airstrikes, Oil Supply Risks and Regional Tensions
item.description: The article details the ongoing escalation of tensions between the United States and Iran, marked by a series of airstrikes targeting Iranian infrastructure and retaliatory strikes from Tehran. U.S. P...

item.title: 10-year-old boy's death highlights systemic hospital transfer failures
item.description: A 10-year-old boy's death has exposed critical failures in the hospital transfer system, according to a recent report. The incident occurred after prolonged delays in transferring the child to a speci...

item.title: House Committee Investigates PFIPC Allegations Amid Budget Concerns
item.description: The House of Representatives ad hoc committee has started investigating the controversial Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC), which lawmakers claim was illegally included in the...

item.title: Liberal Scepticism and the Paralysis of Oppositional Politics in India
item.description: The article critiques the current state of Indian politics, highlighting how liberal scepticism has led to a collapse in the ability to distinguish truth from fiction among opposition groups. It argue...

item.title: Spain's Defensive Mastery Secures 2026 FIFA World Cup Victory
item.description: Spain claimed their second FIFA World Cup title in 2026, defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time at MetLife Stadium. The victory solidified their status as European champions and marked the first time a...
